spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates a symbolization workflow over raw frames, but the v137 stack frames are JS frames (function names + source URLs), not raw addresses needing symbolization. The page is teaching a problem-space that doesn't match the actual report shape.

interactive.rationale: Six buttons drive presets and the symbol map; the JSON output regenerates live.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises an `address -> function symbol map` — but the v137 reports do not contain raw addresses. The blurb encourages the wrong mental model.

edge_cases.rationale: Three presets but no malformed-input handling, no source-map ingestion (the real production workflow), no minified vs un-minified comparison.

references.rationale: Page has NO `see also` or `references` section. Mandatory per CLAUDE.md §3 — without the chromestatus link the slug self-heal cannot recover this folder.

openQuestions.title: Add a references section
openQuestions.detail:
Append `

see also

...
` with at minimum https://chromestatus.com/feature/4731248572628992 (mandatory). Also link the Crash Reporting WICG spec.

openQuestions.title: Reframe symbolization for JS source maps
openQuestions.detail: The v137 stack contains function names and source URLs, not raw addresses. The real-world `symbolize` step is reading a `sourceMap` and unminifying function names. Either pivot the demo to a source-map workflow (paste minified frames + paste a source map → see un-minified names) or relabel as `(applies to Wasm crash addresses)` to set expectations.

openQuestions.title: Add a malformed-input preset
openQuestions.detail: Show what happens with empty frames, malformed JSON, or a symbol map that doesn't cover all addresses.

summary: Solid interactive shell that teaches the wrong concept — v137 reports do not need address symbolization. Reframe around source maps, add the missing references block.
